upgrade_review.upgrade_delta_one_line: The draft names a computation delta, but the multi-neighborhood flow/TU certificate is not yet specified from primitives and the current feasible set has no cross-neighborhood coupling.

repo_prior_art.cite: parent pid_rdd_manipulation_bounded_v1: sharp one-cutoff bounded-manipulation RD endpoint

published_prior_art.cite: gerard_rokkanen_rothe_2020_rdbounds: one-cutoff sharp manipulated-RD bounds, https://doi.org/10.3982/QE1079
closest: gerard_rokkanen_rothe_2020_rdbounds and rosenman_rajkumar_gauriot_slonim_2019_discrete_manipulation give bounds/procedures but not a many-neighborhood flow certificate
closest: bertanha_2020_many_thresholds covers many thresholds, not manipulation-driven TU evaluation
closest: chernozhukov_lee_rosen_2013_intersection_bounds supplies inference for computable bounds, not the RD flow dual strict-gain certificate

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The flagship object G_lambda is promised but §9 never instantiates its node set, arcs, capacities, variable m_j encoding, left-benchmark costs, and budget arcs on a worked multi-neighborhood instance; tier=field below novelty_target=flagship until exhibited.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 2
novelty_flags.one_line: The TU/strongly-polynomial evaluation map is not computed on a laminar two-neighborhood matrix exhibit from §6 primitives.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 3
novelty_flags.one_line: Exhibit 9.3 states a nested witness but does not compute the joint dual value, the separate-envelope benchmark, or the strict gap.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 2
novelty_flags.one_line: The Bertanha comparator is cited without a section/page/equation anchor tying many-threshold RD to the proposed laminar TU gap.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 3
novelty_flags.one_line: The Chernozhukov-Lee-Rosen comparator is cited as an inference consumer without a location anchor for the specific support/intersection-bound object being used.

novelty_flags.label: upgrade
novelty_flags.one_line: PN2 computation is only partially delivered: the delta_summary promises a flow certificate and strongly-polynomial evaluation, but the draft does not yet derive or compute the promised many-neighborhood certificate from primitives.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The stated graph has cell-sink requirements m_j*pi_{j,-}(w) with m_j still variable, and omits how the left-benchmark m_j terms and lower-budget constraints enter the min-cost-flow instance.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 2
soundness_flags.one_line: Laminarity is not connected to the §6 variables: P_{j,+} and U are indexed separately by j, so the displayed feasible set is a product over neighborhoods rather than a shared laminar incidence system.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 3
soundness_flags.one_line: The claimed strict gain over solving neighborhoods separately conflicts with the current §6 separable feasible set; no shared atom or nested-window constraint makes one neighborhood's cap change another neighborhood's retained mass.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Redefine the §6 primitives so nested neighborhoods share atom variables or include explicit consistency/marginalization constraints across windows; otherwise laminarity cannot do mathematical work.
Add a §9 exhibit for J=2 that lists the actual flow network, constraint matrix, dual potentials, joint value, separate-envelope value, and strict gap.
Give location-level anchors for Bertanha and Chernozhukov-Lee-Rosen, and add Ishihara-Sawada 2020 as a nearby manipulation-robust RD diagnostic comparator if it affects the positioning.

State the algorithmic complexity precisely: input size, graph dimensions, cost encoding, whether strongly polynomiality is for fixed lambda only or for endpoint search over lambda, and how breakpoints are enumerated.

recommended_next_step: Revise the setup and exhibits so the many-neighborhood laminar flow object is explicitly constructed and numerically checked before reasserting flagship-tier computation novelty.
